hero.headline: WB Govt Announces Rs 1,000 Crore Infra Development Package For Kolkata
hero.subheadline: Money allocated to improve city's public transport, roads, and water supply
hero.summary: The West Bengal government has unveiled a Rs 1,000 crore infrastructure development package for Kolkata, aiming to improve the city's public transport, roads, and water supply. The move is part of the state government's efforts to enhance the city's liveability and economic competitiveness. The package includes plans to upgrade the city's metro network, construct new roads, and improve water supply infrastructure. The project is expected to be completed within the next two years, with the state government allocating Rs 500 crore in the current fiscal year and Rs 500 crore in the next fiscal year.
hero.impact: Improves Kolkata's liveability and economic competitiveness

stories.headline: Farmers in WB's Hooghly District Benefit from Government's Soil Health Card Scheme
stories.summary: The government's soil health card scheme has helped farmers in Hooghly district increase their crop yields by up to 20%. The scheme provides farmers with a soil health card that suggests the best fertilizers and nutrients required for their crops.

stories.headline: WB Government Launches Initiative to Promote Electric Vehicles in State
stories.summary: The West Bengal government has launched an initiative to promote the use of electric vehicles in the state. The initiative aims to reduce air pollution and promote sustainable transportation. The government has announced a subsidy of up to Rs 10,000 for buyers of electric vehicles.

stories.headline: Teachers in WB's Schools to Get Training on Mental Health Awareness
stories.summary: The West Bengal government has announced plans to provide teachers in state-run schools with training on mental health awareness. The move is aimed at helping students and teachers cope with stress and anxiety.

stories.headline: WB Government to Implement 10% Quota for Economically Weaker Sections in Medica Colleges
stories.summary: The West Bengal government has decided to implement a 10% quota for economically weaker sections in medical colleges in the state. The move is aimed at promoting inclusivity and social justice in the state's medical education system.

stories.topic: Disposal of COVID-19 waste in West Bengal

state_narrative.headline: ABP Ananda: West Bengal government's door-to-door collection of COVID-19 waste
state_narrative.summary: According to ABP Ananda, the West Bengal government has taken steps to collect COVID-19 waste door-to-door to prevent the spread of the disease. The report highlighted the efforts of the state's health department in this regard.
claims_made: West Bengal government is collecting COVID-19 waste door-to-door to prevent the spread of the disease

verified_record.headline: COVID-19 waste disposal in West Bengal: Fact-check
verified_record.summary: According to FactChecker.in, the West Bengal government's claims of door-to-door collection of COVID-19 waste were not entirely accurate. While the state government did collect COVID-19 waste, it was not done door-to-door in all areas. The report relied on official data from the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare.
what_was_omitted: The West Bengal government did not collect COVID-19 waste door-to-door in all areas
verified_record.data_check: Data from the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare's Swachh Bharat Abhiyan (SBA) dashboard showed that while the state government did collect COVID-19 waste, the method of collection varied across different areas.

gap_analysis.key_gap: The West Bengal government's claim of collecting COVID-19 waste door-to-door was not entirely accurate, as the method of collection varied across different areas.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The report from ABP Ananda did not provide information on the variations in COVID-19 waste collection methods across different areas in West Bengal.

stories.topic: Increase in crime rates in West Bengal

state_narrative.headline: Zee News: Rise in crime rates in West Bengal
state_narrative.summary: According to Zee News, there has been a significant increase in crime rates in West Bengal in recent years. The report attributed the rise in crime to the state's deteriorating law and order situation.
claims_made: Crime rates have increased significantly in West Bengal in recent years

verified_record.headline: Crime rates in West Bengal: Fact-check
verified_record.summary: According to IndiaSpend, the crime rates in West Bengal have not increased significantly in recent years. The report relied on data from the National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB) and noted that the state's crime rates were comparable to the national average.
what_was_omitted: The report from Zee News did not provide information on the methodology used to calculate crime rates
verified_record.data_check: Data from the NCRB showed that the crime rates in West Bengal were comparable to the national average, and there was no significant increase in crime rates in recent years.

gap_analysis.key_gap: The report from Zee News exaggerated the increase in crime rates in West Bengal, as the state's crime rates were comparable to the national average.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The report from Zee News did not provide information on the methodology used to calculate crime rates, which could have affected the accuracy of the report.

stories.topic: Implementation of the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY) in West Bengal

state_narrative.headline: ABP Ananda: West Bengal government's efforts to implement PMAY
state_narrative.summary: According to ABP Ananda, the West Bengal government has made significant progress in implementing the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY) in the state. The report highlighted the efforts of the state's housing department in this regard.
claims_made: West Bengal government has made significant progress in implementing PMAY

verified_record.headline: Implementation of PMAY in West Bengal: Fact-check
verified_record.summary: According to Article 14, the West Bengal government's claims of significant progress in implementing PMAY were not entirely accurate. While the state government had made some progress, it was not as significant as reported. The report relied on official data from the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
what_was_omitted: The West Bengal government's progress in implementing PMAY was not as significant as reported
verified_record.data_check: Data from the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs showed that while West Bengal had made some progress in implementing PMAY, it was not as significant as reported by the state government.

gap_analysis.key_gap: The West Bengal government's claim of significant progress in implementing PMAY was not entirely accurate, as the state's progress was not as significant as reported.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The report from ABP Ananda did not provide information on the methodology used to calculate the progress of PMAY implementation in West Bengal.

hottake.headline: LPG prices increase by ₹15 per cylinder in West Bengal
bullets: The price hike affects 150,000 LPG consumers in the state.
According to the IOC, the increased cost is due to global market fluctuations.
This means an additional ₹90 per month for a family of four, depending on usage.

hottake.headline: State government announces 10% wage hike for 1.5 lakh employees
bullets: The move aims to boost purchasing power and stimulate local economy.
The wage increase affects various categories of state government employees.
The Centre will provide ₹200 crore for the wage hike, as per a Cabinet decision.
hottake.source: West Bengal State Government Press Release

hottake.headline: 12 government schools in Kolkata to receive technology infrastructure boost
bullets: The ₹50 lakh project aims to improve digital literacy among 3,000 students.
Upgraded infrastructure includes 40 new computers and 10 smartboards.
The initiative targets underprivileged students in the city's north and south.

hottake.headline: 75 km of arterial roads to be repaired in Kolkata
bullets: The ₹150 crore project includes resurfacing and rehabilitation of damaged roads.
Repair work will be completed within the next six months, as per officials.
The initiative aims to improve road safety and reduce traffic congestion.

hottake.headline: UNVERIFIED: Potential oil spill off West Bengal coast sparks concerns
bullets: Reports suggest a cargo ship may have leaked oil into the Bay of Bengal.
No official confirmation has been issued by the state or central government.
Locals in coastal areas are advised to stay vigilant and report any signs of pollution.

hottake.headline: CONFIRMED: Outbreak of water-borne diseases reported in rural West Bengal
bullets: Cases of cholera and gastroenteritis have been confirmed in two districts.
According to the WHO, 300 people have been affected so far.
The state government has assured that treatment and vaccination efforts are underway.

faq.question: How do I apply for Swasthya Sathi?
faq.answer:
**Swasthya Sathi** is West Bengal's universal health coverage scheme providing ₹5 lakh annual coverage per family.

**Eligibility:** All families in West Bengal with a valid ration card. The card is issued in the name of the woman head of household.

**How to apply:**
1. Visit your nearest **Duare Sarkar** camp (held periodically in every block/ward)
2. Or apply online at **swasthyasathi.gov.in**
3. Bring: Ration card, Aadhaar card, one passport photo
4. The smart card is issued on the spot at camps, or mailed within 15 days for online applications

**What's covered:** Hospitalization, surgeries, diagnostics, medicines at 1,500+ empanelled hospitals across Bengal.

**Helpline:** 1800-345-5384 (toll-free)

*(Source: Department of Health & Family Welfare, Govt. of West Bengal)*

faq.question: What is West Bengal's unemployment rate?
faq.answer:
According to the latest available data:

- **CMIE (April 2026):** West Bengal's unemployment rate is **6.8%**, marginally below the national average of 7.1%
- **PLFS 2024-25:** The state's labor force participation rate is 52.1% (male: 76.2%, female: 26.3%)
- **Youth unemployment (15-29 years):** Estimated at 18.4%, above the national average of 16.2%

**Key context:** West Bengal's female labor force participation rate (26.3%) is significantly below the national average (32.8%), indicating structural barriers to women's workforce entry.

The state's largest employers are agriculture (42%), services (36%), and manufacturing (22%). The informal sector accounts for approximately 89% of total employment.

*(Sources: CMIE Economic Outlook, Periodic Labour Force Survey 2024-25, MOSPI)*

faq.question: How do I file an RTI?
faq.answer:
**Right to Information (RTI)** allows any Indian citizen to request information from government bodies. Here's how:

**Step 1:** Write a simple application addressed to the **Public Information Officer (PIO)** of the relevant department.

**Step 2:** Include:
- Your name and address
- The specific information you're requesting (be precise)
- Write: "I request this information under the Right to Information Act, 2005"

**Step 3:** Pay ₹10 fee (₹0 for BPL card holders) via:
- Court fee stamp, postal order, or demand draft
- Online: **rtionline.gov.in** for central departments
- For WB state departments: Apply at the department office or via registered post

**Timeline:** PIO must respond within **30 days** (48 hours for life/liberty matters).

**If denied:** File a **First Appeal** to the appellate authority within 30 days. Then escalate to the **WB State Information Commission** at wbic.gov.in.

**Helpline:** WB Information Commission — 033-2214-5563

*(Source: RTI Act 2005, WB State Information Commission)*

faq.question: Explain the jute industry crisis
faq.answer:
West Bengal produces **75% of India's raw jute** and houses most of the country's jute mills, primarily along the Hooghly river belt (Howrah, Hooghly, North 24 Parganas).

**The crisis:**
- **Declining demand:** Synthetic alternatives have captured packaging markets that jute traditionally served
- **Aging infrastructure:** Most mills operate on machinery from the 1950s-70s, leading to low productivity
- **Wage disputes:** Workers earn ₹232/day — among the lowest industrial wages in India. Unions demand ₹350+
- **Mill closures:** 12 mills have closed permanently since 2019, displacing ~35,000 workers
- **Raw material price volatility:** Farmers face price crashes during peak harvest, discouraging cultivation

**Government response:**
- **Jute Packaging Material Act** mandates jute packaging for foodgrains and sugar — this keeps ~60% of mills running
- **Jute-ICARE scheme** (₹1,045 crore) for technology modernization
- **MSP for raw jute:** ₹5,350/quintal (2025-26)

**What workers can do:** Contact the Jute Workers' Helpline at the Labour Commissioner's office: 1800-345-4150.

*(Sources: Ministry of Textiles, IJMA Annual Report, The Wire, Indian Jute Mills Association)*

faq.question: What are my rights as a daily wage worker?
faq.answer:
As a daily wage or informal sector worker in West Bengal, you have these legal protections:

**Minimum Wage:**
- WB Minimum Wages Act sets floor wages by industry. Current rates range from ₹232-415/day depending on sector
- **Construction workers:** ₹356/day (skilled), ₹291/day (unskilled)
- Employers MUST pay at least minimum wage — violation is a criminal offense

**Key Rights:**
1. **Equal pay for equal work** — no gender-based wage discrimination (Article 39(d))
2. **MGNREGA:** 100 days guaranteed employment at ₹237/day. Apply at your Gram Panchayat.
3. **WB Building Workers' Welfare Board:** Register for pension (₹1,000/month), medical aid, children's education support
4. **ESI coverage** if your employer has 10+ workers and your salary is below ₹21,000/month
5. **No wage deduction** without written notice and consent

**If your wages are unpaid:**
1. File a complaint at the **District Labour Commissioner** office
2. Call the WB Labour Department helpline: **1800-345-4150**
3. You can also file a case at the **Labour Court** — no lawyer fees required

**Unions:** You have the constitutional right to form or join a trade union (Article 19(1)(c)).

*(Sources: WB Minimum Wages Act, MGNREGA Act 2005, Building & Other Construction Workers Act 1996)*

faq.question: Tell me about the MGNREGA scheme in WB
faq.answer:
**MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act)** guarantees 100 days of wage employment per year to every rural household.

**In West Bengal:**
- **Wage rate:** ₹237/day (2025-26)
- **Person-days generated (2023-24):** 41.3 crore — West Bengal is among the top 5 states by utilization
- **Households benefited:** ~1.2 crore annually

**How to apply:**
1. Visit your **Gram Panchayat** office
2. Submit a written application demanding work (keep a receipt!)
3. Bring: Aadhaar card, bank passbook, passport photo
4. You'll receive a **Job Card** — this is your legal entitlement

**Your rights under MGNREGA:**
- Work must be provided **within 15 days** of application, or you're entitled to an unemployment allowance
- Wages must be paid **within 15 days** of work completion, directly to your bank account
- **Worksite facilities** are mandatory: drinking water, shade, first-aid, crèche for children
- **No contractor system** — work must be executed by the Gram Panchayat

**Common issues in WB:**
- Wage payment delays reported in several districts (average: 25-40 days vs mandated 15)
- Some panchayats show 'ghost beneficiaries' — report this via the Grievance Portal

**Grievance redressal:** nrega.nic.in/netnrega → 'Register Grievance' or call 1800-345-22-44

*(Sources: mgnregs.nic.in, Ministry of Rural Development, CAG Performance Audit 2024-25)*
